category: multi-variant page where the default variant is a small sampler

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. The default selected variant is the 100g Sampler at $8.50 USD, supporting the price and bag-size fields. Boolean/enumerated fields are also supported: filter roast/recommended for filter brewing supports not espresso; no indication of decaf, blend, or co-ferment/infusion; specialty positioning is consistent with the detailed single-farm coffee product listing.

realism.reasons:
This is quite plausible as an html-to-text dump of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is very believable: header nav, roaster name/location, product title, roast type, price, size variants, stock status, tasting notes, origin/producer/process/variety/altitude/harvest fields, descriptive copy, brew recommendations, shipping note, and related/footer links all match common Shopify-style coffee pages. The coffee details are coherent and realistic: Pink Bourbon from Huila, producer Nestor Lasso at El Diviso, washed process, high elevation, and flavor notes like raspberry jam/orange blossom/panela fit the profile. The variant block is also formatted in a believable way with ids, SKUs, prices, and gram weights.

Minor tells keep it from a 5. The currency is a bit odd for a Leeds, UK roaster: showing regular price in USD rather than GBP is possible but not the most natural default. The copy line about the 100g sampler being 'the default size on this page' feels slightly too explanatory/meta compared with typical polished storefront text, as does '[default_for_inference]' in the variant data block, which reads like an annotation rather than something extracted from a real page. 'Harvest: November 2024' is not impossible, but depending on current listing timing it can feel slightly over-specified or synthetic. Overall, though, it is close to real and only has minor artifacts.

label.coffee.sensory_text: raspberry jam, orange blossom, panela, silky
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: raspberry jam, orange blossom, panela, silky
label.coffee.producer_text: This Pink Bourbon lot comes from Nestor Lasso and the team at Finca El Diviso in Bruselas, Huila. Cherries were hand-picked at peak ripeness, floated and carefully fermented before washing and slow drying on raised beds. The cup is bright and layered: raspberry jam up front, a clean orange-blossom florality, and a panela sweetness that carries through the finish.
